cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the best title for this passage about body language in the office. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the main points. The passage starts by talking about how body language is powerful and that it communicates a lot more than words. Then it goes into specific tips about what not to do in the office. The tips include not touching co-workers, not avoiding eye contact, having a good handshake, and not leaning your head while listening. The main focus seems to be on avoiding mistakes in body language specifically in an office setting. Looking at the answer choices now: A) Pay attention to your body language at the office. B) How to get along with your co-workers. C) The meaning of body language you should know. D) Different countries have different body language. Option A directly mentions paying attention to body language at the office, which aligns with the passage's focus on specific office-related body language tips. Option B is about getting along with co-workers, which is a broader topic. While the passage does mention avoiding mistakes that cause trouble, the main focus is on body language, not general social skills. Option C talks about the meaning of body language, but the passage is more about what not to do rather than explaining the meanings of different body languages. It does mention some meanings (like eye contact showing trust), but the main thrust is about behavior to avoid. Option D discusses different countries and their body languages, which is not covered in the passage. The passage is about general office body language,
